en.name: Lymphocytes in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Lymphocytes are the leukocytes of the adaptive immune system (typically 20–40 % of circulating leukocytes), comprising T cells, B cells and NK cells [1]. The absolute lymphocyte count is part of the CBC differential and is used to assess infection (e.g. lymphocytosis in viral infection, lymphopenia in HIV, sepsis or steroid therapy) and haematological disorders. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a haematology analyser as part of the differential white-cell count, on whole blood (EDTA tube). It is collected in routine biochemistry and intensive care. Results are reported as an absolute number concentration, typically in **`10*9/L`** (NPU SI convention, equivalent to `G/L`) or in the US notation **`10*3/uL`** (numerically identical: `1 ×10⁹/L = 1 ×10³/µL`). ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on the LOINC Group `40654045 / LG32863-9 — Lymphocytes|Number Concentration (count/vol)|Moment in time|Blood (LOINC Group)` and pulls in all its descendants. This resolves to three standard concepts, which differ only by counting method. ### Generic (method not specified) - *3019198 / 26474-7 — Lymphocytes [#/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: most widely used LOINC code, returned by haematology analysers when the method is not detailed. ### Method-specific - *3004327 / 731-0 — Lymphocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count (LOINC)*: automated impedance, optical or flow-cytometric count (most common in routine practice). - *3003215 / 732-8 — Lymphocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Manual count (LOINC)*: manual differential on a stained smear under microscope (used as a confirmation when the analyser flags the result, or in low-resource settings). **Note on units.** Recommended unit: **`10*9/L`** (`9444 / 10*9/L — billion per liter (UCUM)`), aligned with NPU [2]. LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS` is `10*3/uL` (`8848 / 10*3/uL — thousand per microliter (UCUM)`), which is the same physical quantity in US notation. Conversion: `1 ×10⁹/L = 1 ×10³/µL` (factor 1). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded. The set is defined by anchoring on the LOINC Group for lymphocyte number concentration in blood and pulling in all its descendants. This naturally excludes: - **Lymphocyte subsets** — T cells (CD3, CD4, CD8), B cells (CD19, CD20), NK cells (CD56), regulatory T cells, etc. — out of scope. These are captured under flow-cytometry concept sets if needed. - **Percentage / fraction variants** — lymphocytes as a fraction of total leukocytes (`Lymphocytes/100 leukocytes`, `Lymphocytes/Leukocytes [Pure number fraction]`) — out of scope, separate measurand. - **Atypical / variant / reactive lymphocytes** — these are reported as separate measurands (qualitative or quantitative), out of scope. - **Other specimens**: cord blood, fetal blood, body fluid, bone marrow, CSF, dialysis fluid, peritoneal/pleural/synovial fluid, stem cell product — out of scope for a "blood" concept set. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "Lymphocytes", "Lympho", "LYM#" or similar absolute count without specifying the method: `3019198 / 26474-7 — Lymphocytes [#/volume] in Blood`. By far the most common. - **Method-specific source**: if the source explicitly states "automated" or "manual", map to the corresponding method-specific concept. Otherwise, default to `3019198 / 26474-7 — Lymphocytes [#/volume] in Blood`. - **Source reports a percentage** (e.g. `25 %` of leukocytes): this is **not** the same measurand. It belongs to a different concept set (lymphocyte percentage). - **Unit notation**: source systems may report `10*9/L`, `G/L`, `10*3/uL`, `K/uL`, or `/uL` (×1000). All correspond to the same physical quantity and can be converted to the recommended `10*9/L` (factor 1 with `10*3/uL`, factor 0.001 with `/uL`). - **Do not confuse with**: lymphocyte percentage, T/B/NK subset counts, atypical lymphocytes, or lymphocytes on non-blood specimens — all belong to other concept sets. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 26474-7 — Lymphocytes [#/volume] in Blood (Part description for "Lymphocytes"). Available: https://loinc.org/26474-7/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
